page: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server configured with an AMD EPYC 4345P processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, utilizing a dedicated allocation model. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of 3890 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. No GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression and decompression tasks scoring in the top 10%. Passmark memory scores also rank in the top tier. However, multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput fall into the average tier. A notable performance tradeoff is the large-block write memory bandwidth, which is positioned in the bottom 25% of benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers a high density of dedicated CPU resources and substantial NVMe storage without the overhead of virtualization. It is well-suited for workloads requiring strong single-threaded execution, local storage-heavy operations, and moderate memory requirements. Appropriate use cases include relational databases, file compression pipelines, static web serving, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 4345P processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 3890 GB NVMe SSD local storage array, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU execution and memory scores, alongside average multi-core and database performance. However, large-block memory write bandwidth represents a performance tradeoff, landing in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile delivers cost-efficient dedicated resources suitable for storage-heavy workloads, single-threaded processing pipelines, and database hosting.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-64gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a 16-core AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB RAM, and 3890 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and general workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining high-speed single-core AMD EPYC processing with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
